title: Sprint 2 — Session Continuity and Context Architecture
description: Adds session continuity and tiered context architecture to the harness: git recovery with checkpoint strategy, memory compaction to prevent long-term memory degradation, structured machine-readable handoffs, and a docs/ directory as the second tier of project context. Evals, observability, and HITL were removed from scope — redundant for CLI-based usage with Claude Code and OpenCode.

acceptanceCriteria.description: skills/git-recovery.md exists with clean state definition, checkpoint procedure, bisect/revert/reset recovery steps, session handoff via WIP commits, and guardrails
skills/memory-compaction.md exists with when-to-run triggers, 8-step compaction procedure, promotion path to rules/, and guardrails
skills/handoff-format.md exists with frontmatter schema (persona, task, status, timestamp), all conditional sections, status definitions, and guardrails
docs/README.md exists with directory structure, file conventions including mandatory 'What Agents Must Know' section, index table, and maintenance rules
skills/boot.md updated with docs/ tier step and memory compaction trigger
personas/coder.md updated with git-recovery checkpoint step and handoff-format delegation
skills/dispatch.md updated to always include git-recovery and handoff-format for all dispatches

notes: Scope reduced from original Sprint 2 plan. Evals, observability, and HITL skills removed — Reviewer persona + spec status already covers eval quality gate; Claude Code/OpenCode CLIs provide native observability; CLI usage means user is always in the loop.
